mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136018.0 thousand as of May 2012
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% while inflation-adjusted real rates stay negative, equity valuations expand as the opportunity cost of equity capital declines relative to cash holdings, producing sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% and real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI YoY) < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of May 2012 does not satisfy trigger
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary accommodation with financial repression
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1.5% signals weakening consumer demand and deteriorating near-term earnings visibility, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices reprice downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368861.0 million as of May 2012
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal exhibits Rank IC below 0.01 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment remains elevated above 4.0%, signaling supply-side weakness and constrained capacity utilization that limits corporate profit expansion and equity returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: sluggish recovery with labor market slack and supply constraints
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are simultaneously met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction signal exhibits Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, signaling a contraction in residential investment and forward-looking construction demand that reduces near-term GDP growth expectations and equity valuations over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1486.0 thousand units as of April 2012
regime_hint: housing market contraction or demand shock
failure_condition: When trigger condition is met, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the signal is below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period while payroll employment simultaneously declines, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136018.0 thousand as of May 2012
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary shock
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal exhibits Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating negative real rates that erode fixed-income returns and drive equity risk premium compression, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of May 2012 does not satisfy trigger
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal is below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while retail sales RSAFS simultaneously contract month-over-month, signaling broad-based demand weakness that compresses equity earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11450.5 billion as of January 2012
fact:RSAFS at 368861.0 million as of May 2012
failure_condition: When both trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction signal exhibits Rank IC below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.
